name: North cardinal buoy
meaning: Pass to the north of this mark; safe water lies to the north.

invariants.distinguishing: black above yellow; two black cone topmarks both point up; safe-water side is north of the mark

geometry.basis: Generate fresh owned SVG from the Chart No.1 crop and style primitives. Use chartsymbols.xml only for object-class lookup, CATCAM identity, pivot/origin sanity checks, palette-token expectations, and sibling discrimination; do not copy OpenCPN raster artwork into the owned pack.

primitive_plan.name: cardinal_band_north
primitive_plan.semantic_role: black above yellow cardinal colour pattern

primitive_plan.name: cone_topmark_pair_up
primitive_plan.semantic_role: two black cones both points up
primitive_plan.style_axis: stroke weight/corner treatment only; orientation must not vary

acceptance_checks: SVG references CSS variables for black and yellow, with no literal hex colours.
Verifier sees two cone topmarks both pointing up.
Sibling forced-choice returns BOYCAR_north, not BOYCAR_south, BOYCAR_east, or BOYCAR_west.
Atlas manifest entry remains keyed by name/kind/palette plus style axis.
